title: Podman rmi very slow for both single image delete and for all image delete
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
podman rmi imageid -f, takes a lot of time to remove only one image and if we use podman rmi -a -f, takes hours to remove all the images

We run podman as rootless.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run podman rmi command for a single or all images.

**Describe the results you received:**
time podman rmi df123ccdd678 -f
Deleted: df123ccdd678f3ad5a34226f451d5651263c61605e77cad23cf7ff1d11129d5e

real    11m7.606s
user    0m2.140s
sys     0m7.720s

**Describe the results you expected:**
it should run faster.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Thu Jul 29 10:02:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.4 (Ootpa)

comments.body: And when "podman rmi -af" is running, the "podman build" command waits for the "rmi" command to finish first. At least let me know how can I run "podman build" command parallel if the "podman rmi" command is in process?

If you are reporting a bug on RHEL operating systems, please report them in the RH bugzilla system -> https://bugzilla.redhat.com/

There is clearly something wrong/different with your image storage.  Generally, rmi is impacted only by the number of images you have in your store and the size of those images.  For example, here is me deleting a 6.5GB image on my laptop:

```
time podman rmi quay.io/coreos-assembler/coreos-assembler
Untagged: quay.io/coreos-assembler/coreos-assembler:latest
Deleted: f6bcef5d1fe47d99fbf8dbb2fb170cacb44febd43e4ac10d7830e2c62f19c5a2

real	0m3.865s
```

Build and rmi cannot be run at the same time because rmi takes a lock on the image storage.  Perhaps this is what you are seeing.

There are other factors that can impact speed.  Obviously disk i/o speeds, networked directories, as well as specific storage options, and finally a corrupted image store.  If there is nothing important to you in terms of containers and images, I would first try a `podman system reset --force` which will blow away your image and container store.  Then try again.

If you feel there is an actual bug here, please file the bugzilla so it can be addressed properly.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no further feedback and this is not something upstream can easily fix, I am closing.
